name: Fernet
creator: Python cryptography library team
breakability: Relies on AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256. No known practical attack.
modern_use: Python application secrets, config encryption, API token storage
description: Fernet is a high-level symmetric authenticated encryption scheme from the Python cryptography library. It uses AES-128-CBC for encryption and HMAC-SHA256 for authentication. The key is a URL-safe base64-encoded 32-byte value. Fernet is designed to be hard to misuse.
notes: Fernet tokens include a timestamp. Use MultiFernet for key rotation.
did_you_know: Fernet gets its name from the Italian digestif liquor - it was named as a playful nod to something that goes down smoothly.
